title: Ohm's Law
description: Explore V = IR. Adjust voltage or resistance and watch current change in real time.

steps.instruction: This circuit has a 9 V source and a 1 kΩ resistor forming a simple loop. The simulator shows current flowing through the resistor.
steps.hint: Current direction follows conventional flow: out of the positive terminal, through the circuit, back to the negative terminal.

steps.instruction: Apply Ohm's Law: I = V / R. With V = 9 V and R = 1 kΩ, calculate the expected current.
In the Falstad simulator above, right-click the resistor and set its value to 4.5 kΩ. Observe how the current changes.
steps.hint: I = 9 V / 4500 Ω = 2 mA. Doubling resistance halves the current.

steps.instruction: Try different values. Notice that current and resistance are inversely proportional when voltage is fixed.
steps.hint: This is the essence of Ohm's Law: V = IR is always satisfied in a linear resistive circuit.
